title: IF/SWITCH inside iterator expression — callback risk
description: IF or SWITCH in the expression argument of an iterator (SUMX, AVERAGEX, etc.) forces row-by-row Formula Engine evaluation. The engine cannot batch-evaluate conditional logic across rows.
fix_suggestion: Move the conditional logic outside the iterator using VAR, or restructure with CALCULATE

title: IFERROR/ISERROR inside iterator — callback risk
description: IFERROR or ISERROR in the expression argument of an iterator forces step-by-step error checking for every row, completely preventing SE optimization.
fix_suggestion: Handle the error condition explicitly before the iterator, or use DIVIDE for division errors

title: Nested iterators — O(n*m) callback risk
description: An iterator inside another iterator (e.g., SUMX inside SUMX) creates O(n*m) row-by-row evaluation. This scales quadratically with table size.
fix_suggestion: Flatten with SUMMARIZECOLUMNS, or use CALCULATE to avoid the inner iteration

title: FILTER over bare table reference
description: FILTER with a bare table name (not wrapped in ALL/VALUES/etc.) iterates the table under the current filter context. This is often correct but can unexpectedly scan large tables.
fix_suggestion: Consider whether you need FILTER(ALL(Table), ...) or FILTER(VALUES(Table[Col]), ...) for explicit intent

title: FORMAT forces FE evaluation
description: FORMAT is not SE-pushable and forces the entire expression into the single-threaded Formula Engine. Use measure FormatString or client-side formatting.
fix_suggestion: Use the measure's FormatString property or report-level formatting instead

title: LASTDATE — prefer MAX for date comparison
description: LASTDATE returns a single-row table, which can cause unexpected behavior in comparisons. MAX returns a scalar date value, which is simpler and more predictable.
fix_suggestion: Replace LASTDATE(DateColumn) with MAX(DateColumn) for scalar date comparisons
